revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:古楠神社 (掛川市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | Name = Furukusu Jinja | Image =
境内のギョイコウ（2016年4月） | Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|qid=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}{{ill|Nishi-Ōbuchi|de|Nishi-Ōbuchi|ja|西大渕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Ōbuchi|1=Nishiōbuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132011940}}174番地
{{Maplink2 |display = inline |type = point |title = 古楠神社 |description = 古楠神社 |marker = marker |zoom = 16 |frame = yes |plain = yes |frame-align = center }} | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 42 | Latitude seconds = 0.89104 | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 41.72911 | Enshrined kami = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred object = | Rank = | Foundation = 701年 | Main hall = | Alternative name = | Fuda-sho etc. = | Annual festival = 6月第2日曜日 | Shinto rituals = | Map = Japan Shizuoka#Japan | image= File:Furukusu Jinja 201604early 1.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Kakegawa|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.70024750650055|N|137.9615914180317|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} '''Furukusu Jinja''' (古楠神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|qid=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}. ==Origins== Originally, it enshrined the “Furukusu Tennō,” who arrived as an accompanying deity when {{ill|Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)|de|Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|lt=Mikumano Shrine|lt_ja=三熊野神社|lt_de=Mikumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=}} was transferred from Kumano in Kishū. Until around 1942 (Shōwa 17), there was no main hall—only stone paving and a sacred tree. When the hall was built and the stone paving removed, a magatama was unearthed. It is now carefully kept as a treasure of Mikumano Shrine. Some say that even before Mikumano Shrine was moved here, this place was already a sacred area where a deity was worshipped.「大須賀町誌」を参照 ==References== * 大須賀町誌編纂委員会 『大須賀町誌』 静岡県小笠郡大須賀町 ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} --> [[File:Furukusu Jinja 201604early 1.jpg|300px]] [[File:Furukusu Jinja 201604early 2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|境内のギョイコウ（2016年4月）]] {{translated page|ja|古楠神社 (掛川市)|version=103595468|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:古楠神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:古楠神社]] [[de:Furukusu Jinja]] {{Commons category|Furukusu Jinja (Kakegawa)}} {{wikidata link|Q11411305}} [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
